sections.label: Section 5.8.998b
sections.original: Non pol temere est quod tu tam times .
sections.translation: It isn ’ t for nothing , i ’ faith , that you are in such a fright .

sections.label: Section 5.8.999
sections.translation: What , I in a fright ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.999b
sections.translation: All right , of course : since you are not in a fright at all ,

sections.label: Section 5.8.1000
sections.original: Et hoc nihil est quod ego dico , tu narra .
sections.translation: and this is nothing at all that I ’ m going to tell , do you relate it .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1000b
sections.translation: Villain , is he to relate it at your request ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1001b
sections.original: Eho tu , factum est abs te sedulo
sections.translation: Come now , you ’ ve managed nicely for your brother .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1002b
sections.translation: My husband , will you not tell me ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1002c
sections.translation: But —

sections.label: Section 5.8.1002d
sections.translation: But what ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1003
sections.translation: There ’ s no need to tell you .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1003b
sections.original: Tibi quidem : at scito huic opus est .
sections.translation: Not for you , indeed ; but there ’ s need for her to know it . At Lemnos —

sections.label: Section 5.8.1004b
sections.translation: Ha ! what are you doing ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1004c
sections.translation: Won ’ t you hold your tongue ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1004d
sections.translation: Unknown to you —

sections.label: Section 5.8.1004e
sections.translation: Ah me !

sections.label: Section 5.8.1005
sections.translation: He married another —

sections.label: Section 5.8.1005b
sections.translation: My dear sir , may the Gods forbid it !

sections.label: Section 5.8.1006
sections.translation: Such is the fact .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1006b
sections.translation: Wretch that I am , I ’ m undone !

sections.label: Section 5.8.1006c
sections.translation: And had a daughter by her , too , while you never dreamed of it .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1007b
sections.translation: What are we to do ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1008
sections.original: Pro Di immortales , facinus indignum , et malum .
sections.translation: O immortal Gods !— a disgraceful and a wicked misdeed !

sections.label: Section 5.8.1009
sections.translation: It ’ s all up with you .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1009b
sections.original: An quicquam hodie est factum indignius ?
sections.translation: Was ever any thing now more ungenerously done ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1010
sections.original: Qui mihi ubi ad uxores ventum est tum fiunt senes .
sections.translation: Your men , who , when they come to their wives , then become incapacitated from old age .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1011
sections.original: Demipho , te appello ; nam me cum hoc ipso distaedet loqui .
sections.translation: Demipho , I appeal to you ; for with that man it is irksome for me to speak . Were these those frequent journeys and long visits at Lemnos ? Was this the lowness of prices that reduced our rents ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1014
sections.original: Ego , Nausistrata , esse in hac re culpam meritam non nego ;
sections.translation: Nausistrata , I don ’ t deny that in this matter he has been deserving of censure ;

sections.label: Section 5.8.1015
sections.translation: but still , it may be pardoned .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1015b
sections.translation: He is talking to the dead .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1016
sections.original: Nam neque negligentia tua , neque odio id fecit tuo .
sections.translation: For he did this neither through neglect or aversion to yourself . About fifteen years since , in a drunken fit , he had an intrigue with this poor woman , of whom this girl was born , nor did he ever touch her afterward . She is dead and gone : the only difficulty that remained in this matter .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1020
sections.original: Quamobrem te oro , ut alia facta tua sunt , aequo animo hoc feras .
sections.translation: Wherefore , I do beg of you , that , as in other things , you ’ ll bear this with patience .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1021
sections.original: Quid ego aequo animo ? Cupio misera in hac re iam defungier .
sections.translation: Why should I with patience ? I could wish , afflicted as I am , that there were an end now of this matter . But how can I hope ? Am I to suppose that , at his age , he will not offend in future ? Was he not an old man then , if old age makes people behave themselves decently ? Are my looks and my age more attractive now , Demipho ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1025
sections.original: Quid mihi nunc affers quamobrem exspectem aut sperem porro non fore ?
sections.translation: What do you advance to me , to make me expect or hope that this will not happen any more ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1026
sections.original: Exsequias Chremeti quibus est commodum ire hem tempus est .
sections.translation: Those who have [*] a mind to come to the funeral of Chremes , why now ’ s their time . ’ Tis thus I retaliate : come now , let him challenge Phormio who pleases : I ’ ll have him victimized [*] with just a like mischance . Why then , let him return again into her good graces . I have now had revenge enough .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1030
sections.original: Habet haec ei quod dum vivat usque ad aurem ogganniat .
sections.translation: She has got something for her as long as she lives , to be forever ringing into his ears .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1031
sections.original: At meo merito , credo : quid ego nunc commemorem , Demipho ,
sections.translation: But it was because I deserved this , I suppose ; why should I now , Demipho , make mention of each particular , how I have conducted myself toward him ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1032b
sections.translation: I know it all , as well as yourself .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1033b
sections.translation: Does it appear , then , that I deserved this treatment ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1033c
sections.translation: Far from it : but since , by reproaching , it can not now be undone ,

sections.label: Section 5.8.1035
sections.original: Ignosce : orat ; confitetur ; purgat : quid vis amplius ?
sections.translation: forgive him : he entreats you — he begs your pardon — owns his fault — makes an apology . What would you have more ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1036
sections.original: Enimvero prius quam haec dat veniam , mihi prospiciam et Phaedriae .
sections.translation: But really , before she grants pardon to him , I must take care of myself and Phaedria . ( To NAUSISTRATA .) Hark you , Nausistrata , before you answer him without thinking , listen to me .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1037b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1038
sections.original: Ego minas triginta per fallaciam ab illo abstuli ;
sections.translation: I got out of him thirty minae by a stratagem . I give them to your son ; he paid them to a Procurer for his mistress .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1040
sections.translation: Ha ! what is it you say ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1040b
sections.original: Adeone hoc indignum tibi videtur , filius
sections.translation: Does it seem to you so very improper for your son , a young man , to keep one mistress , while you have two wives ? Are you ashamed of nothing ? With what face will you censure him ? Answer me that .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1043
sections.translation: He shall do as you wish .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1043b
sections.original: Immo , ut meam iam scias sententiam ,
sections.translation: Nay , that you may now know my determination , I neither forgive nor promise any thing , nor give any answer ,

sections.label: Section 5.8.1045
sections.original: Prius quam gnatum video : eius iudicio permitto omnia ;
sections.translation: before I see my son : to his decision I leave every thing . What he bids me , I shall do .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1046b
sections.translation: You are a wise woman , Nausistrata .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1047
sections.translation: Does that satisfy you , Chremes ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1047b
sections.original: Immo vero pulchre discedo et probe ,
sections.translation: Yes , indeed , I come off well , and fully to my satisfaction ; indeed , beyond my expectation .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1048b
sections.original: Tu tuum nomen die quod est .
sections.translation: Do you tell me , what is your name ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1048c
sections.translation: What , mine ? Phormio ; a well-wisher to your family , upon my honor , and to your son Phaedria in particular .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1050
sections.original: Phormio , at ego ecastor posthac tibi quod potero et quae voles
sections.translation: Then , Phormio , on my word , henceforward I ’ ll both do and say for you all I can , and whatever you may desire .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1051b
sections.translation: You speak obligingly .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1051c
sections.translation: I ’ faith , it is as you deserve .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1052
sections.original: Vin primum hodie facere quod ego gaudeam , Nausistrata ,
sections.translation: First , then , will you do this , Nausistrata , at once , to please me , and to make your husband ’ s eyes ache with vexation ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1053b
sections.translation: With all my heart .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1053c
sections.translation: Invite me to dinner .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1054
sections.translation: Assuredly indeed , I do invite you .
